title: Execution Model v2 — Phase 2: planning-aware tk next/graph + stale-state support
description:
ROUGH SCOPE — flesh out into ticks when Phase 1 (azw) closes. Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 (tk changes), §3 (stale state recovery), Open Question 4.

- tk next / tk graph: an unblocked epic with no children is returned as a planning action (additive 'action: plan' field in --json; human-readable output may change freely).
- Stale in_progress support: a way to list ticks stuck in_progress (for the skill's resume/recovery pass that resets them to open).
- Likely code: internal/query, cmd/tk/cmd/next.go, graph command, tests in internal/query.
notes:
2026-06-10 15:14 - RETRO REPORT — Phase 2 (hbz)
Learnings promoted: .tick/learnings.md +1 entry (define consumer-facing fields by consumer semantics, not implementation predicate — the needs_planning blocker). One-offs stay in tick notes.
Outside-in verification (dogfooded on this repo): EpicsNeedingPlanning predicate VERIFIED (zk7/kgh childless cases); tk next action field VERIFIED (implement/plan/await on live ticks); tk graph needs_planning VERIFIED incl. blocked->false after fix; tk list last_activity VERIFIED (42w showed real 18m age).
Final review: 1 blocker (needs_planning ignored blocked state — JSON/test fixed), 3 should-fix (all-closed-children misreport fixed; awaiting path got action:await; O(kxM) activity scans replaced by single-pass bulk variant), 3 nits (2 fixed: unused param, bufio scanner; 1 accepted: listOutput shape divergence is documented in code).
Known nit carried forward: tk graph's 'epic complete — close it' hint also shows for ALREADY-closed epics (graph on closed epic edge); fix opportunistically in phase 3/4.
Proposed roadmap adjustments: none.
closed_reason: All 5 ticks completed via Claude orchestration (3 waves + review-fix round). Retro report in epic notes.
